QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_29

question.text: A merchant banker has a liquid net worth of Rs. 15 crore. What is the maximum total underwriting obligation it can assume?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 22, total underwriting obligations shall not exceed 20 times the liquid net worth. Therefore, Rs. 15 crore × 20 = Rs. 300 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 150 crore (10 times) is tempting because 10x multiples are common in financial regulations. Rs. 225 crore (15 times) is another plausible but incorrect multiplier. **Cross-Exam Context:** Capital adequacy multipliers are tested in NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) for clearing members and NISM Series V-A for AMC net worth. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always maintain a buffer — if your liquid net worth drops even temporarily, your underwriting obligations may breach the 20x limit.
